capability: A7 — Decision tree at project kickoff / framework-fit check (core, 5/7 SOPs)
stance: neutral; frameworks-are-layers-not-competitors; surfaces disagreements rather than crowning a winner

operations.name: Do-you-even-need-a-framework gate (per layer)
operations.trigger: Before adopting any framework on any layer
operations.action: Confirm raw SDK is insufficient. L1 raw-wins: 1 call, no metric, verbatim audit, rapid iter. L2 raw-wins: corpus <100k tokens & static. L3 raw-wins: control flow fits if/else, no state, no HITL.

operations.name: Layer identification (Pass A)
operations.trigger: Project kickoff; before any pip install
operations.action: Walk the stack; mark L1/L2/L3/L6/L7 + coding-surface needed or not for THIS project.

operations.name: L1 Modeling pick rubric
operations.action: raw prompts default; DSPy at ≥2 calls + ≥30 examples + metric + swaps; Outlines/Guidance for grammar guarantees; avoid same-layer collision with LangChain prompts.

operations.name: L2 Retrieval pick rubric
operations.action: LlamaIndex default; Haystack for classical IR; RAGFlow when retrieval quality is THE bottleneck; raw vector store only if ≤2 primitives; Aider repo-map (not embeddings) for code corpora.

operations.name: L3 Orchestration pick rubric
operations.action: Gate via agent-topology-selection first (~80% single-agent). Then: LangGraph for cycles/durable/HITL/time-travel/parallel; CrewAI for whiteboard roles + demo speed; Workflows for retrieval-heavy + some agency; AutoGen only if debate-is-product (maint-mode).

operations.name: L6 Serving pick (delegate)
operations.action: Hand off to llm-engine-selection. Summary: vLLM default; SGLang prefix-heavy; TensorRT-LLM NVIDIA-locked+budget; llama.cpp CPU/edge/≤1user; Ollama dev. Hosted API → skip L6.

operations.name: L7 App-platform pick rubric
operations.action: Dify for scaffolding + mixed-role; Flowise for single chatbot; LangFlow for export-to-Python; n8n for automation-first. Plan code-escape hatch; mind ceiling.

operations.name: Coding-agent surface pick (gate first)
operations.trigger: Deliverable is code edits in a repo
operations.action: Gate via repo-state-gating (existing repo vs greenfield). Then Aider (terminal/git/scriptable) / Cline (VS Code approval) / Cursor (visual) / Continue (cross-IDE) / OpenHands (autonomous).

operations.name: Interop check (Pass C)
operations.action: Confirm pieces compose. Additive: DSPy-in-node, retriever-as-tool, Dify+LangGraph-behind-HTTP, vLLM via OpenAI-compatible API. Re-pick on same-layer collisions.

dilemma_cases.name: LangGraph vs CrewAI (graph vs role)
dilemma_cases.resolution: Whiteboard test (named teammates, no branching → CrewAI) vs durability test (cycles/crash/multi-tenant/HITL/time-travel → LangGraph). Standard arc: prototype CrewAI → port LangGraph. No clean crossover rubric (conceded).

dilemma_cases.name: Framework vs raw SDK (when raw wins)
dilemma_cases.resolution: Run G0 per layer. Raw wins: 1 call no-metric (L1); <100k static corpus (L2); if/else control flow no-HITL (L3). DSPy line-count is not a reason to compile a task that doesn't need it.

dilemma_cases.name: Dify visual vs code framework (the ceiling)
dilemma_cases.resolution: Start visual when scaffolding-bottleneck + mixed-role; plan code-escape up front; migrate at ceiling (>40-50 nodes / HITL needed / >10 QPS-pod / extreme RAG). Thresholds Dify-specific, directional only.

dilemma_cases.name: LlamaIndex vs LangGraph for agentic RAG (which layer leads)
dilemma_cases.resolution: Let dominant difficulty lead. Messy-docs/retrieval-quality → build around LlamaIndex, add LangGraph when agency emerges. Multi-step/many-tools/durable-state → build around LangGraph, embed LlamaIndex retrievers as a tool.

anti_patterns.name: Framework-driven development (pick before knowing problem)
anti_patterns.fix: Pass A first; framework choice is downstream of layer map

anti_patterns.name: Same-layer framework collision (e.g. DSPy + LangChain prompts)
anti_patterns.fix: one per layer; mixed same-layer adoption is a smell

anti_patterns.name: Visual-builder past its ceiling
anti_patterns.fix: planned code-escape hatch, not more nodes

anti_patterns.name: Treating serving as an orchestration choice
anti_patterns.fix: L6 is a different layer; defer to llm-engine-selection

anti_patterns.name: Adopting maintenance-mode tools blind (AutoGen/Swarm/TGI)
anti_patterns.fix: adoption-risk is a first-class selection criterion
